━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
📚 DAILY MARKET EDUCATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🏦 CENTRAL BANK POLICIES: YOUR TRADING COMPASS

Central banks control the money supply and interest rates. These decisions directly impact currency values and market direction.

When the Fed raises rates, the dollar typically strengthens. Higher rates attract foreign investment seeking better returns. When ECB cuts rates, the euro often weakens as capital flows elsewhere.

REAL EXAMPLE: In March 2022, the Fed began aggressive rate hikes from 0.25% to 5.25%. The USD Index surged 15% in 9 months. Traders who understood this policy shift captured massive trends.

Key policy tools you must watch:

- Interest rate decisions (8 scheduled meetings per year for major banks)
- Quantitative easing or tightening (balance sheet size)
- Forward guidance (what central bankers signal about future moves)
- Emergency interventions (rare but market-moving)

How to trade central bank events:

- Mark all rate decision dates on your calendar
- Read the policy statement, not just the rate change
- Watch the press conference for tone shifts
- Trade the trend that follows, not the initial spike

PRO TIP: The expectation matters more than the actual decision. If markets already priced in a rate hike, the announcement may cause little movement. Look for surprises in the guidance or vote split.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🧠 REVENGE TRADING: THE SILENT ACCOUNT KILLER

Revenge trading happens when you take impulsive trades to recover recent losses. This emotional response destroys more accounts than any technical mistake.

The psychology is simple but deadly. After a loss, your brain craves immediate recovery. You abandon your strategy, increase position size, and force trades that don't meet your criteria.

REAL SCENARIO: You lose 2% on a EUR/USD trade. Instead of following your plan, you immediately enter GBP/JPY with double size to make it back quickly. No setup, no analysis, pure emotion. You lose another 4%. Now you're down 6% and emotionally wrecked.

Warning signs you're revenge trading:

- Taking trades immediately after a loss
- Increasing position size without reason
- Trading markets you normally avoid
- Ignoring your entry criteria
- Feeling angry or desperate

How to break the revenge cycle:

- Set a hard rule: No trading for 30 minutes after any loss
- Close your platform and take a physical break
- Review the losing trade in your journal
- Accept that losses are business expenses, not personal failures
- Remember: One good setup is worth ten forced trades

SMART MONEY RULE: Professional traders view each trade independently. Your account doesn't care about individual wins or losses. It only cares about your statistical edge over 100 trades.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

💡 THREE WEEKEND TRADING TIPS

TIP 1: REVIEW YOUR WEEKLY PERFORMANCE
Saturday is analysis day. Calculate your win rate, average win vs average loss, and total return. Identify patterns in your winners and losers. What setups worked? What time of day produced best results? Data reveals truth that emotions hide.

TIP 2: PREPARE FOR MONDAY GAP RISK
Weekend news can create gaps at Monday open. Reduce position sizes held over weekends or close Friday before the close. If holding, set wider stops to avoid gap stop-outs. Check economic calendar for Sunday evening releases that might trigger volatility.

TIP 3: STUDY ONE LOSING TRADE DEEPLY
Pick your worst loss this week. Screenshot the chart and mark exactly where you entered and why. What did you miss? Was the setup actually valid? Did you follow your rules? Learning from losses builds expertise faster than celebrating wins.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

⚠️ MONEY MANAGEMENT: THE 2% RULE EXPLAINED

Never risk more than 2% of your account on a single trade. This simple rule is the difference between surviving drawdowns and blowing up accounts.

THE MATH THAT SAVES CAREERS:

With 2% risk per trade, you can survive 20 consecutive losses and still have 66% of your capital. With 10% risk per trade, five losses destroy half your account.

Example with 10,000 account:
- 2% risk = 200 per trade maximum loss
- Stop loss placed so that if hit, you lose exactly 200
- Position size adjusts based on stop distance
- Not based on gut feeling or confidence level

How to calculate position size:

- Account size: 10,000
- Risk per trade: 2% = 200
- Entry: 1.1000
- Stop loss: 1.0950
- Stop distance: 50 pips
- Position size: 200 divided by 50 pips = 0.40 lots

Common mistakes to avoid:

- Using the same lot size for every trade regardless of stop distance
- Risking more because you feel confident
- Recovering mode risk increases after losses
- Forgetting to account for spread and slippage

PRO TIP: As your account grows, your position sizes grow automatically with the 2% rule. A 20,000 account risks 400 per trade. This compounds your gains while protecting your capital. Discipline with 2% turns small accounts into substantial ones over time.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Educational purpose only
